id: SD-015-trace\\n depends_on:\\n - SD-015\\n - FEAT-006\\n---\\n# Resolution Path Trace: CLI Flag → HTTP Request\\n\\n**Companion doc to** [SD-015 Agent Routing and Catalog Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) and [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md).\\n\\nSD-015 and SD-023 describe *intent* — what the routing layer should do across\\nfive routing modes. This document describes *reality* — how one concrete CLI\\ninvocation flows through every layer of the code today, with file:line\\ncitations. It exists so a newcomer debugging a routing bug can read one\\ndocument instead of reverse-engineering the flow from `routing.go`,\\n`discovery.go`, `agent_runner.go`, `runner.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`.\\n\\n**Author's invocation used throughout this trace:**\\n\\n```bash\\nddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\\n```\\n\\n**Citations are anchored to base revision `654b4cdcd9200fa8db6c38d6af9dad2acfbaa779` (2026-04-18).**\\nLine numbers drift as the code evolves — see the *CI citation check* section\\nat the end for how this document is kept in sync.\\n\\n## Current Gaps (as of 2026-04-18)\\n\\nEach gap links to its tracking bead. Update this table when a bead closes or a\\nnew structural defect is discovered.\\n\\n| # | Gap | Visible at | Tracking bead |\\n|---|-----|------------|---------------|\\n| 1 | `RouteRequest` has no `Provider` field; `--provider` is silently dropped during normalization unless it names a registered harness. | `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` (struct def); `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` (only consumed as harness alias) | [ddx-8610020e](#) — routing: add Provider field to RouteRequest |\\n| 2 | `RunOptions.Provider` is a parallel path consumed by `resolveHarness()` and `resolveNativeAgentProvider()` — bypasses the routing layer entirely for the embedded-agent harness. | `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382`; `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` | [ddx-2f5a2284](#) — ModelRef/Profile paths must consult discovery for `--provider` + catalog override |\\n| 3 | `FuzzyMatchModel` does no case-folding and no canonical-prefix normalization. `qwen/qwen3.6` will not match `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit` because the slash, vendor prefix, and case are treated literally. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` | [ddx-0486e601](#) — fuzzy matcher lacks case + canonical-prefix normalization |\\n| 4 | `scoreCandidate` has no provider-affinity bonus: when two harnesses can serve the same discovered model, the scorer does not prefer the harness whose provider actually advertised the model. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` | [ddx-3c5ba7cc](#) — tier escalation must respect `--provider` affinity |\\n| 5 | Fuzzy-match tiebreak picks shortest-suffix, not highest-version. `qwen3` against `[qwen3.5-27b, qwen3.6-35b]` picks `qwen3.5`. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` | [ddx-0216b966](#) — discovery fuzzy match tiebreak |\\n| 6 | No pre-dispatch capability check — context window, tool support, effort, permissions. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` (`rejectIfNotViable` covers availability only) | [ddx-4817edfd](#) — pre-dispatch capability check |\\n\\n## Sequence Diagram\\n\\n```mermaid\\nsequenceDiagram\\n actor User\\n participant CLI as cli/cmd/work.go + agent_cmd.go\\n participant Norm as NormalizeRouteRequest
(routing.go:289)\\n participant Disc as DiscoverProviderModels
(discovery.go:124)\\n participant Plan as BuildCandidatePlans
(routing.go:27)\\n participant Fuzzy as FuzzyMatchModel
(discovery.go:66)\\n participant Score as scoreCandidate
(routing.go:477)\\n participant Sel as SelectBestCandidate
(routing.go:556)\\n participant Run as Runner.Run
(runner.go:141)\\n participant RunAgent as Runner.RunAgent
(agent_runner.go:65)\\n participant Resolve as resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider
(agent_runner.go:464)\\n participant AgentLib as agentlib.Run
(agent_runner.go:320)\\n participant HTTP as Provider HTTP endpoint\\n\\n User->>CLI: ddx work --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\\n CLI->>Norm: RouteFlags{...}\\n Note over Norm: --provider consumed only
if it names a registered harness
(GAP #1)\\n Norm-->>CLI: RouteRequest{ModelPin, Profile}\\n CLI->>Disc: DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)\\n Note over Disc: 30s process-wide cache\\n Disc-->>CLI: DiscoveryResult{Providers[]}\\n CLI->>Plan: BuildCandidatePlans(req, states)\\n loop each harness\\n Plan->>Plan: evaluateCandidate\\n Plan->>Plan: rejectIfNotViable\\n end\\n Plan->>Fuzzy: FuzzyMatchModel (when uncataloged)\\n Fuzzy-->>Plan: resolved model ID\\n Plan->>Score: scoreCandidate per plan\\n Plan-->>Sel: []CandidatePlan (ranked)\\n Sel-->>CLI: best plan\\n CLI->>Run: Runner.Run(RunOptions)\\n Run->>RunAgent: if harnessName == \\\"agent\\\"\\n RunAgent->>Resolve: resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider(wd, model, provider, modelRef)\\n Resolve->>Disc: second-pass lookup (cached)\\n Resolve-->>RunAgent: {Provider, BaseURL, APIKey, Model}\\n RunAgent->>AgentLib: agentlib.Run(ctx, Request{...})\\n AgentLib->>HTTP: POST /v1/chat/completions\\n HTTP-->>AgentLib: stream | unexpected EOF\\n AgentLib-->>RunAgent: Result\\n RunAgent-->>User: Result (stdout + session log)\\n```\\n\\n## Layer 1 — CLI flag parse\\n\\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\\nfrom the inner command.\\n\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\\n are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\\n `effort`, `local`).\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\\n it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\\n runs before any bead is claimed.\\n\\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\\n`RunOptions`:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\\n constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\\n\\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\\n\\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\\n\\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\\n initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \\\"\\\"` branch assigns\\n `HarnessOverride` directly.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \\\"\\\"` branch: the\\n provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\\n `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\\n value is silently discarded.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \\\"\\\"` branch routes the\\n model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\\n in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\\n `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\\n **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\\n`{ModelPin: \\\"qwen/qwen3.6\\\"}`.\\n\\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\\n TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours. The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance. PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists. P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell. P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop. P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers. P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it. P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e). P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads. NOT IN SCOPE - Code changes (this bead is doc-only) - Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children) INTERSECTIONS - Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces - Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads EVIDENCE - ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality. .ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/prompt.md:14:Evidence cross-link: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and serves as evidence for P7 / bead-quality. .ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/prompt.md:16: phase:2, area:agent, area:server, kind:design, reliability, bead-quality .ddx/executions/20260505T125509-42f63724/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM Per epic ddx-a9d130d0 + ADR-023 + sub-skill bead-lifecycle/: ddx try needs to invoke the sub-skill at two points (pre-dispatch lint, post-attempt triage) and act on the structured output. Audit-1 (commit 86848d21) covered 20 beads; audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) covered 108 beads; 88 of 108 scored ≤5 against the rubric. Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap. ROOT CAUSE Today nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all. PROPOSED FRAMEWORK A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill. NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code. CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases) 1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy) 2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples 3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field 4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline) After child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead). NON-SCOPE - ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking) - ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run) - Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model) - in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality) GOVERNANCE - ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/) - FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks) - FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records - FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists) - FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed) .ddx/executions/20260505T105911-3023859b/prompt.md:13: phase:2, area:beads, area:agent, kind:platform, reliability, bead-quality, adr:023 .ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/manifest.json:3: "bead_id": "ddx-06b77652", .ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/manifest.json:10: "id": "ddx-06b77652", .ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/manifest.json:12: "description": "The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.\n\nThe 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.\n\nPRINCIPLE STATEMENTS\n\nP1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.\n\nP2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.\n\nP3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see \"preflight skipped (no operator pin)\" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.\n\nP4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.\n\nP5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.\n\nP6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).\n\nP7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)\n- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)\n\nINTERSECTIONS\n- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces\n- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads\n\nEVIDENCE\n- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.", .ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/manifest.json:21: "bead-quality" .ddx/executions/20260505T141415-de904250/manifest.json:98: "worktree": "tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-06b77652-20260505T141415-de904250" .ddx/executions/20260505T074136-36b65cad/manifest.json:3: "bead_id": "ddx-06b77652", .ddx/executions/20260505T074136-36b65cad/manifest.json:10: "id": "ddx-06b77652", .ddx/executions/20260505T074136-36b65cad/manifest.json:12: "description": "The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours. The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance. PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists. P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell. P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop. P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers. P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it. P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e). P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads. Root cause RC2 from ddx-0a651925\n\nThe `DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000` (2h) in `cli/internal/agent/types.go:363` is advertised as a hard cap but implemented as an *idle timeout*:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/executor.go:154-178` resets `time.NewTimer(idleTimeout)` on every stdout/stderr byte.\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:264-285` resets on every non-compaction agentlib event.\n\nA provider that emits any heartbeat, retry line, or periodic event defeats the timer indefinitely — enabling the 33–142h hangs observed on agent projects.\n\nOnly the claude streaming path (`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:373-384`) uses a true wall-clock deadline via `time.After(timeout)`. That path is why bundle `20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba` fired at exactly 2h 0m 0.098s — and nothing else does.\n\n## Scope\n\n1. Add an absolute wall-clock deadline in addition to (not replacing) the idle timeout.\n2. Default the wall-clock bound to something operators can trust — e.g. 3x `DefaultTimeoutMS` or a new `DefaultWallClockMS`.\n3. Apply in: `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` (subprocess path) and `Runner.RunAgent` (embedded agent path).\n4. Reflect the wall-clock error in result.json as a distinct `error: \"wall-clock deadline exceeded after Xh\"` so it is not confused with idle timeout.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Lowering or removing the existing idle timeout.\n- Per-provider tuning (can be follow-up).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-18T05:29:50.370737575Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent Conventional commit ending [\u003cthis-bead-id\u003e].","closing_commit_sha":"baf5f748920779c3379712225341fe58ec9be46c","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.651384066Z","description":"The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.\n\nThe 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.\n\nPRINCIPLE STATEMENTS\n\nP1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.\n\nP2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.\n\nP3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see \"preflight skipped (no operator pin)\" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.\n\nP4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.\n\nP5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.\n\nP6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).\n\nP7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)\n- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)\n\nINTERSECTIONS\n- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces\n- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads\n\nEVIDENCE\n- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.","events_attachment":"ddx-06b77652/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"exit status 1","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-05-05T04:20:59Z","id":"ddx-06b77652","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:server","kind:design","reliability","bead-quality"],"notes":"Evidence cross-link: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and serves as evidence for P7 / bead-quality.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-249cd7a8","status":"closed","title":"ADR-style: 7 reliability principles for ddx try / ddx work / sub-agent execution","updated_at":"2026-05-05T14:19:39.552645934Z"} .ddx/beads.jsonl:122:{"acceptance":"1. 4 stale duplicate beads closed:\n - ddx-6af3ae8f OR ddx-8835a765 closed as duplicate (operator picks which to keep)\n - ddx-155204fd OR ddx-cb63cdfc closed as duplicate\n - ddx-4fc9be2e closed with reference to ddx-d8474e0e\n Each closure includes --notes citing the audit recommendation.\n\n2. 40 HIGH-priority beads from audit-2 §\"High-priority retrofit list\" retrofitted. Two competing hypotheses for why:\nH1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.\nH2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.\n\nBoth are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.\n\nTHREE PATHS\n\nPath A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).\nPath B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.\nPath C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.\n\nINTERPRETATION\n- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).\n- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).\n- A == B \u003c\u003c C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.\n\nMETHOD\nPick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.\n\nMEASUREMENTS\nPer path per bead:\n- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)\n- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)\n- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)\n- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored\n- Cost: tokens, USD\n- Manual interventions\n- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list\n\nOUTPUT\n.ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:\n- Per-bead per-path table\n- Aggregate stats (median + p99)\n- Failure-mode distribution\n- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)\n- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape\n\nTIMING\nThis study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)\n- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)\n- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)\n\nEVIDENCE\n- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.","id":"ddx-31f745cd","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:tooling","kind:study","reliability","bead-quality"],"notes":"Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)","updated_at":"2026-05-05T03:08:30.860761453Z"} .ddx/beads.jsonl:294:{"acceptance":"1. Today the loop records status/detail/rationale but has no triage hook invocation path that can populate OutcomeReason.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:940-1090 contains the outcome adjudication and cooldown branch chain. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:976 and 1084 call shouldSuppressNoProgress before applying no-progress cooldown. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1099 appends the report to the loop result after cooldown handling. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:30 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PostAttemptTriageHook and cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go defines TriageResult, but no cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go exists and the loop never calls the hook.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n - Add cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go with a constructor, e.g. NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot string, store BeadReader, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, sessionLogReader SessionLogExcerptReader) func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error). If package boundaries require a different name, preserve the explicit injected inputs.\n - The hook builds a prompt whose first line is MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes the bead body, ExecuteBeadReport as JSON, the most relevant recent bead events, and a bounded session-log excerpt when report.SessionID is non-empty and a log can be found under the project agent log dir.\n - Invoke dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: prompt, WorkDir: projectRoot, PromptSource: \"bead-lifecycle-triage\"}) or an equivalent package-local wrapper that still uses AgentRunner/ResolvedConfig and not os/exec.\n - In execute_bead_loop.go, call runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook after report.Status/Detail/Rationale/BaseRev/ResultRev are final for the attempt and before any shouldSuppressNoProgress or SetExecutionCooldown branch. A valid classification sets report.OutcomeReason = TriageResult.Classification and records triage details in the execute-bead event body or a dedicated bead-quality.triage event.\n - On hook failure, fail open: leave OutcomeReason empty and keep legacy cooldown behavior.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n - Defining OutcomeReason and TriageResult; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.\n - CLI wiring for ddx try; covered by sibling/parent wiring work.\n - GraphQL exposure of OutcomeReason.\n - Automatically creating SuggestedFollowupBeads; record/surface only unless a later bead opts in.\n\nPARENT\n ddx-e1a576a7.\n\nDEPS\n No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and the OutcomeReason/TriageResult contract Today many beads punt the investigation, omit concrete test names, and leave non-scope implicit. Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been compensating; this bead surfaces the gap and produces a template.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nBeads were authored expecting the executor to investigate first. Sub-agents (and ddx try / ddx work workers) need investigation done UPFRONT to execute reliably. Without it, the executor either:\n- Gives up (no_changes after limited investigation), OR\n- Produces wrong work (wrong file, wrong scope), OR\n- Asks for clarification (not possible in autonomous drain)\n\nROOT CAUSE 2\nNo formal authoring template exists. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md describe bead types but not bead structure or quality bar. New beads inherit whatever the operator was thinking at the moment.\n\nSCOPE\n1. Audit 20 representative open beads (mix of priorities, areas, types) against a quality rubric.\n2. Identify common gaps; quantify (how many beads miss test names, how many lack file:line cites, etc.).\n3. Produce bead-authoring-template.md at docs/helix/06-iterate/.\n4. Update CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and DDx skill at .agents/skills/ddx/ to reference the template.\n5. Identify which 10-15 high-impact open beads warrant retrofit (the next round of execution work depends on them).\n\nQUALITY RUBRIC (the audit measures each bead against)\n- (a) Title: one-line scope clarity\n- (b) Description: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope\n- (c) AC #1-N: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names\n- (d) AC includes 'wired-in' assertion when introducing new code paths\n- (e) AC includes go test command + lefthook gate\n- (f) Labels: phase + area + kind + cross-refs (adr/spec/prevention)\n- (g) Parent + Deps: explicit\n- (h) Description reads as sufficient sub-agent prompt (no operator hand-curation needed)\n\nScore per bead: 8/8 = passes; less = needs retrofit.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- Retrofitting beads (separate follow-up beads filed from the audit's recommendations)\n- Auto-validating bead structure on creation (future tooling bead)\n- Reorganizing existing bead labels or hierarchies\n\nINTERSECTIONS\n- RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead (this audit operationalizes P7)\n- Retrofit recommendations from this audit gate the dispatch of refactor children C4-C13 (bead retrofit MUST land before sub-agent dispatch runbook","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T06:17:17Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"d810ddff35013426b0e3ec92f4e1ad1fcb45c777","created_at":"2026-04-15T18:59:41.811906799Z","description":"Execution bundles under .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/ are the primary forensic record of what DDx automation did, but the most valuable part — the per-iteration agent trace at embedded/agent-*.jsonl — cannot reasonably be checked in. Individual traces can exceed 50 MB; a busy automation day produces hundreds of MB. Axon hit this on 2026-04-15 when GitHub warned about a 50.18 MB embedded trace file, and embedded/ is now gitignored per-repo.\n\nWe still want that level of detail for *every* run, for post-mortems, routing/model analysis, and training-data curation. We need a configurable out-of-band mirror.\n\n## Proposed shape\n\n1. **Archive target** configured via `.ddx/config.yaml` (and/or env var):\n - `executions.mirror.kind: local | s3 | gcs | http`\n - `executions.mirror.path: s3://bucket/ddx-executions/{project}/{attempt_id}/` (template with `{project}`, `{attempt_id}`, `{date}`, `{bead_id}`)\n - `executions.mirror.include: [manifest, prompt, result, usage, checks, embedded]` (allow excluding embedded for bandwidth-sensitive setups, but default = everything)\n - `executions.mirror.async: true` (upload in background after execute-bead returns so it never blocks the hot path)\n\n2. **When to mirror**: at execute-bead finalization, after result.json is written. On failure, retry async with bounded attempts; log failures to `.ddx/agent-logs/mirror.log` but do not fail the bead.\n\n3. **Index**: each mirrored bundle gets a pointer row appended to a local `.ddx/executions/mirror-index.jsonl` (attempt_id, bead_id, mirror_uri, uploaded_at, byte_size). This gives analysts a quick `jq` lookup without walking the remote store.\n\n4. **Retrieval**: `ddx agent executions fetch \u003cattempt_id\u003e` pulls a bundle back to .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt_id\u003e/ for local inspection.\n\n5. **GC**: optional local retention policy (`executions.retain_days`) that prunes old local bundles while the mirror keeps the full history.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Not a full audit trail service — just a mirror of DDx's existing bundles.\n- Not a replacement for git-tracked manifest/prompt/result/usage summaries — those stay checked in.\n- Not a distributed log aggregator — one-shot copy per bundle, no streaming.\n\n## Why now\n\nAxon's ADR-018 session produced ~85 MB of execution artifacts in a single day; only 1.5 MB of that is useful as checked-in summary. Without a mirror, every project using DDx execute-bead faces the same tension: either gitignore the traces and lose the forensic data, or commit them and bloat the repo But subsequent commits land in the source tree, and the operator forgets to rebuild. New flags / subcommands appear in source but silently disappear from the operator's CLI surface. Today's session bit this twice: 'ddx work plan --limit' worked, then later returned 'unknown flag: --limit' — not because the feature was broken, but because the binary was stale relative to source.\n\nOBSERVED (2026-05-03 session)\n- Built + installed at commit c0f95380; 'ddx work plan --limit' worked.\n- ~10 commits later: 'ddx work plan --limit' returned 'unknown flag.'\n- Source still had the flag definition; binary was stale.\n- Operator wasted time investigating a non-existent regression before remembering to rebuild.\n\nThis compounds because:\n1. Source can drift hours-to-days ahead of the installed binary during active development.\n2. Cobra's 'unknown flag' / 'unknown command' errors give NO hint that the binary is stale.\n3. ddx work auto-spawns workers — those workers also use the stale binary, propagating the gap.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/cmd/version.go implements 'ddx version' and 'ddx --version'. It prints the build-time-injected version string (e.g., 'v0.6.2-alpha12-12-g86848d21') from ldflags but does NOT compare against the source tree's current HEAD when invoked from inside a ddx project repo. No stale-binary detection exists in cli/cmd/doctor.go either. The build SHA is injected at link time via main.go -ldflags but never compared against runtime git rev-parse HEAD.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nExtend 'ddx --version' (or add 'ddx doctor' check) to:\n1. Detect when invoked from inside a git repo whose origin matches the ddx project (DocumentDrivenDX/ddx).\n2. Compare the binary's build SHA (from -ldflags / main.go version injection) against the repo's HEAD.\n3. If HEAD is ahead of the binary's SHA, print a warning to stderr: 'WARNING: installed ddx is built from \u003cbinary-sha\u003e; source tree HEAD is \u003chead-sha\u003e (\u003cN\u003e commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.'\n4. Exit 0 (informational, not fatal).\n\nOptional: emit same warning on EVERY ddx command invocation when inside the ddx project (cheap: one git rev-parse).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Auto-rebuilding (operator should explicitly rebuild).\n- Comparing against upstream remote (only local source tree).\n- Detection from outside the ddx project Existing FEAT docs are stale on relevant points:\n- FEAT-011 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:180 says DDx ships exactly one skill, but .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md already demonstrates nested sub-skill packaging.\n- FEAT-004 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:150 owns bead validation hooks but doesn't describe authoring-quality lint or post-attempt triage.\n- FEAT-010 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:21 documents the layer-1/2/3 invocation model but doesn't spec hook insertion points for ddx try / ddx work.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Author ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md covering:\n - Policy: lint pre-dispatch + triage post-attempt; both invoke the same bead-lifecycle sub-skill\n - Staged rollout: WARN-ONLY default → BLOCK opt-in after baseline confirms\n - Waiver model: rubric-level by bead-type (doc/epic/deletion skip certain criteria, per docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:219); per-bead lint-waiver:\u003ccriterion\u003e label for rare exceptions; --force --reason records an event (does not silently bypass)\n - Fail-open hook behavior (P1 from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES): infrastructure failure proceeds; only valid low lint score blocks (in BLOCK mode)\n - Operator recovery UX: blocked dispatch prints missing fields + suggested ddx bead update commands\n\n2. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime additions (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go ~line 30):\n PreDispatchLintHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)\n PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)\n LintResult struct { Score int; Rationale string; SuggestedFixes []string; WaiversApplied []string }\n TriageResult struct { Classification string; RecommendedAction string; Rationale string; SuggestedAmendments string; SuggestedFollowupBeads []FollowupBead }\n\n2. PreDispatchLintHook fires AFTER nextCandidate, BEFORE Claim (sibling to RoutePreflight at execute_bead_loop.go:662).\n - Hook implementation in cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go: composes runner library to invoke bead-lifecycle skill in lint mode (per FEAT-001/FEAT-010 layer-1 invocation; NOT subprocess via ddx agent run per codex).\n - WARN-ONLY behavior (default): record lint result as bead event (kind=bead-quality.lint); proceed with claim regardless of score.\n - BLOCK mode (opt-in via config flag in .ddx/config.yaml): refuse claim when score \u003c threshold; emit operator-readable explanation + cite bead-lifecycle SKILL.md guidance for fixes.\n - FAIL-OPEN per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES P1: infrastructure failure (skill missing, harness unavailable, bad JSON, timeout) → emit warning event + proceed. Only valid low-score response blocks (and only in BLOCK mode).\n\n3. PostAttemptTriageHook fires AFTER outcome adjudication, BEFORE cooldown decision (read execute_bead_loop.go ~line 880-1100 for the outcome class chain).\n - Hook implementation in cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go: composes runner library to invoke bead-lifecycle skill in triage mode with (bead + outcome event + session log excerpt).\n - Triage classification populates new ExecuteBeadReport.OutcomeReason field (string).\n - shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1522 extends to also short-circuit when OutcomeReason ∈ {transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict} (transient classes per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES P6). Hook invocation cost: lint runs once per dispatch attempt (cheap sonnet call); triage runs once per non-clean outcome. Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) and [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md).\\n\\nSD-015 and SD-023 describe *intent* — what the routing layer should do across\\nfive routing modes. This document describes *reality* — how one concrete CLI\\ninvocation flows through every layer of the code today, with file:line\\ncitations. It exists so a newcomer debugging a routing bug can read one\\ndocument instead of reverse-engineering the flow from `routing.go`,\\n`discovery.go`, `agent_runner.go`, `runner.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`.\\n\\n**Author's invocation used throughout this trace:**\\n\\n```bash\\nddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\\n```\\n\\n**Citations are anchored to base revision `654b4cdcd9200fa8db6c38d6af9dad2acfbaa779` (2026-04-18).**\\nLine numbers drift as the code evolves — see the *CI citation check* section\\nat the end for how this document is kept in sync.\\n\\n## Current Gaps (as of 2026-04-18)\\n\\nEach gap links to its tracking bead. Update this table when a bead closes or a\\nnew structural defect is discovered.\\n\\n| # | Gap | Visible at | Tracking bead |\\n|---|-----|------------|---------------|\\n| 1 | `RouteRequest` has no `Provider` field; `--provider` is silently dropped during normalization unless it names a registered harness. | `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` (struct def); `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` (only consumed as harness alias) | [ddx-8610020e](#) — routing: add Provider field to RouteRequest |\\n| 2 | `RunOptions.Provider` is a parallel path consumed by `resolveHarness()` and `resolveNativeAgentProvider()` — bypasses the routing layer entirely for the embedded-agent harness. | `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382`; `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` | [ddx-2f5a2284](#) — ModelRef/Profile paths must consult discovery for `--provider` + catalog override |\\n| 3 | `FuzzyMatchModel` does no case-folding and no canonical-prefix normalization. `qwen/qwen3.6` will not match `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit` because the slash, vendor prefix, and case are treated literally. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` | [ddx-0486e601](#) — fuzzy matcher lacks case + canonical-prefix normalization |\\n| 4 | `scoreCandidate` has no provider-affinity bonus: when two harnesses can serve the same discovered model, the scorer does not prefer the harness whose provider actually advertised the model. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` | [ddx-3c5ba7cc](#) — tier escalation must respect `--provider` affinity |\\n| 5 | Fuzzy-match tiebreak picks shortest-suffix, not highest-version. `qwen3` against `[qwen3.5-27b, qwen3.6-35b]` picks `qwen3.5`. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` | [ddx-0216b966](#) — discovery fuzzy match tiebreak |\\n| 6 | No pre-dispatch BuildCandidatePlans\u003cbr/\u003e(routing.go:27)\\n participant Fuzzy as FuzzyMatchModel\u003cbr/\u003e(discovery.go:66)\\n participant Score as scoreCandidate\u003cbr/\u003e(routing.go:477)\\n participant Sel as SelectBestCandidate\u003cbr/\u003e(routing.go:556)\\n participant Run as Runner.Run\u003cbr/\u003e(runner.go:141)\\n participant RunAgent as agentlib.Run\u003cbr/\u003e(agent_runner.go:320)\\n participant HTTP as Provider HTTP endpoint\\n\\n User-\u003e\u003eCLI: ddx work --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\\n CLI-\u003e\u003eNorm: RouteFlags{...}\\n Note over Norm: --provider consumed only\u003cbr/\u003eif it names a registered harness\u003cbr/\u003e(GAP #1)\\n Norm--\u003e\u003eCLI: RouteRequest{ModelPin, Profile}\\n CLI-\u003e\u003eDisc: DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)\\n Note over Disc: 30s process-wide cache\\n Disc--\u003e\u003eCLI: DiscoveryResult{Providers[]}\\n CLI-\u003e\u003ePlan: BuildCandidatePlans(req, states)\\n loop each harness\\n Plan-\u003e\u003ePlan: evaluateCandidate\\n Plan-\u003e\u003ePlan: rejectIfNotViable\\n end\\n Plan-\u003e\u003eFuzzy: FuzzyMatchModel (when uncataloged)\\n Fuzzy--\u003e\u003ePlan: resolved model ID\\n Plan-\u003e\u003eScore: scoreCandidate per plan\\n Plan--\u003e\u003eSel: []CandidatePlan (ranked)\\n Sel--\u003e\u003eCLI: best plan\\n session log)\\n```\\n\\n## Layer 1 — CLI flag parse\\n\\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\\nfrom the inner command.\\n\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\\n are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\\n `effort`, `local`).\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\\n it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\\n runs before any bead is claimed.\\n\\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\\n`RunOptions`:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\\n constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\\n\\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\\n\\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\\n\\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\\n initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \\\"\\\"` branch assigns\\n `HarnessOverride` directly.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \\\"\\\"` branch: the\\n provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\\n `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\\n value is silently discarded.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \\\"\\\"` branch routes the\\n model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\\n in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\\n `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\\n **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\\n`{ModelPin: \\\"qwen/qwen3.6\\\"}`.\\n\\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\\n TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only\\n way to force a re-probe in tests.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in\\nthe catalog:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the\\n discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != \\\"\\\" \u0026\u0026 req.ModelRef == \\\"\\\" \u0026\u0026 r.Discovery == nil`.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`,\\n `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised\\n the requested model.\\n\\n## Layer 4 — Candidate planning\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the\\n viability gate. Checks:\\n - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:399` — `!s.Installed`.\\n - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:402` — `!s.Reachable`.\\n - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:405` — `!s.Authenticated`.\\n - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:408` — quota exhausted.\\n - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:411` — degraded.\\n - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:414` — policy violation.\\n - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:418` — harness override mismatch.\\n\\nNote: none of these check *capability* — context window, tool support, effort\\nlevel support. That is Gap #6.\\n\\n## Layer 5 — Fuzzy matching\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:66` — `FuzzyMatchModel(input)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:70` — delegates to `fuzzyMatchInPool` using\\n `d.AllModels()` (a flat deduplicated pool across all providers — not per-provider).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` — `fuzzyMatchInPool(input, pool)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:78` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:82` — exact-match short-circuit.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:89` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:96` — prefix match: candidate `m` is considered if `len(m) \u003e len(input) \u0026\u0026 m[:len(input)] == input`. **The comparison is case-sensitive and treats slashes literally (Gap #3).**\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:106` — tiebreak: shortest suffix wins; alphabetical on equal suffix length. **Not version-aware (Gap #5).**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\nPool likely contains `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`. Input `qwen/qwen3.6` fails the\\nexact check, then fails the prefix check because `Q != q` and the slash is not\\nin the pool entries. Today's surfaces:\n- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead\n- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled\n- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log\n\nThis blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.\n\nCode today (relevant entry points):\n- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate\n- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAdd a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:\n\n ddx try \u003cbead-id\u003e [--harness=\u003cname\u003e] [--model=\u003cname\u003e] [--profile=\u003cname\u003e]\n\nBehavior:\n1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.\n2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.\n3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):\n STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.\n STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.\n Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.\n4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).\n5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)\n- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)\n- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)\n- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)\n- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)\n\nINTERSECTION\n- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.\n- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.\n- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nToday nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all.\n\nPROPOSED FRAMEWORK\nA SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill.\n\nNO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code.\n\nCHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases)\n1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy)\n2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples\n3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field\n4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline)\n\nAfter child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking)\n- ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run)\n- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model)\n- in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality)\n\nGOVERNANCE\n- ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/)\n- FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks)\n- FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records\n- FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists)\n- FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed)", .ddx/executions/20260505T041444-ae32c3e7/prompt.md:88:+ "bead-quality", .ddx/executions/20260505T035807-0bbef293/prompt.md:40: phase:2, area:agent, kind:feature, reliability, bead-quality, adr:023 .ddx/executions/20260505T035807-0bbef293/prompt.md:90:+ "bead-quality", .ddx/executions/20260505T041450-ff52383f/manifest.json:12: "description": "PROBLEM\nPer epic ddx-a9d130d0 + ADR-023 + sub-skill bead-lifecycle/: ddx try needs to invoke the sub-skill at two points (pre-dispatch lint, post-attempt triage) and act on the structured output. Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.\n\nThe 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.\n\nPRINCIPLE STATEMENTS\n\nP1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.\n\nP2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.\n\nP3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see \"preflight skipped (no operator pin)\" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.\n\nP4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.\n\nP5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.\n\nP6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).\n\nP7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). PROPOSED FRAMEWORK A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill. NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code. CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases) 1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy) 2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples 3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field 4. Today's surfaces:\n- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead\n- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled\n- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log\n\nThis blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.\n\nCode today (relevant entry points):\n- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate\n- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAdd a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:\n\n ddx try \u003cbead-id\u003e [--harness=\u003cname\u003e] [--model=\u003cname\u003e] [--profile=\u003cname\u003e]\n\nBehavior:\n1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.\n2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.\n3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):\n STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.\n STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.\n Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.\n4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).\n5. Code today (relevant entry points): - cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner) - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim PROPOSED FIX Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go: ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>] Behavior: 1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found. 2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies. 3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler): STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run. STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing). 5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr). ID:       ddx-f339c399
Title:    evidence: ddx-29058e2a no_changes outcome validates P7 (bead-quality > execution-machinery hypothesis)
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 3
Labels:   phase:2, area:beads, kind:evidence, reliability, bead-quality
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-06b77652
Desc:     EVIDENCE
2026-05-04 02:24:15: 'ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex' returned no_changes after one attempt. Bead description was NOT in the retrofit batch (audit recommended it but it was held back to test exactly this prediction). Same codex, same machinery, succeeded earlier on retrofit'd beads ddx-aee651ec and ddx-3e60fd84.

This is strong evidence for the P7 (bead-as-prompt) hypothesis from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652): bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success, possibly more dominant than the execution machinery itself.

CONTRAST
- Non-retrofit ddx-29058e2a (multi-file scope, 'see also' references, no concrete starting point): no_changes
- Retrofit ddx-aee651ec (concrete file paths, named types): success at commit ?? (closed 2026-05-03 20:46)
- Retrofit ddx-3e60fd84 (file:line cites, named tests): success at commit ?? (closed 2026-05-03 22:24)

CONCLUSION
The 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path B-vs-C question (sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated) has a partial answer already: when bead is well-authored, sub-agent + verbatim bead succeeds; when bead is poorly authored, even capable executors return no_changes.

NEXT STEPS
1. Retrofit ddx-29058e2a per audit recommendations (separate operator work)
2. Re-dispatch ddx-29058e2a via codex after retrofit
3. If retrofit'd ddx-29058e2a succeeds: P7 hypothesis fully validated; comparison study Path B-vs-C answered
4. Audit + retrofit remaining open beads on the dispatch path (so future dispatches don't waste codex usage on no_changes outcomes)

NON-SCOPE
- Closing ddx-29058e2a (separate retrofit + re-dispatch)
- Modifying the 3-path comparison study (this is partial evidence; full study still planned post-refactor)
- Changing the bead-authoring template (no template change indicated yet)
Accept:   1. This bead exists with the evidence above committed to the bead store.
2. Linked from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead (ddx-06b77652) as evidence for P7.
3. Linked from comparison-study bead (ddx-31f745cd) as partial Path B-vs-C data.
4. Closed once the post-retrofit re-dispatch of ddx-29058e2a confirms P7 hypothesis.

ID:       ddx-31f745cd
Title:    study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:agent, area:tooling, kind:study, reliability, bead-quality
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Deps:     ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178
Desc:     Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.
Accept:   1. 5 fixture beads chosen and listed in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/comparison-fixtures.md with: bead ID, base-rev, original closing commit, characterization (trivial/refactor/test/multi-file/docs).
2. Path A run: each fixture's bead replayed via ddx try (or ddx work --once) on a branch from base-rev~1; outcomes captured.
3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.
4. Path C run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with operator hand-curated prompt; outcomes captured.
5. Per-fixture, per-path measurements table with the 7 named metrics.
6. Aggregate stats + failure-mode distribution.
7. Hypothesis verdict section: H1 / H2 / both / inconclusive.
8. Recommendations section: which path for which bead-shape.
9. Report committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md.
10. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).
