ID:       ddx-06b77652
Title:    ADR-style: 7 reliability principles for ddx try / ddx work / sub-agent execution
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:agent, area:server, kind:design, reliability, bead-quality
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.
Accept:   1. docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md exists and contains all 7 principles with their statements above (verbatim).
2. README sections of each refactor child (C4, C5, C6, C7, C8, C9, C11, C12, C13) cite the principle(s) the child enforces. Cross-link in those bead descriptions added via ddx bead update.
3. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md updated with one-line pointers: 'See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.'
4. cd cli && go test ./... still green (no code change; sanity check that nothing broke).
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
6. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].

  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.","id":"ddx-06eb05d8","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result","updated_at":"2026-05-05T07:43:36.356850587Z"}

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.880567652Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-358f2457","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c8f79963","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-b669bb9f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-848069a3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-9228a484","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-0b8780db","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c670ef0a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-387a0178","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"}],"description":"Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.","id":"ddx-31f745cd","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:tooling","kind:study","reliability","bead-quality"],"notes":"Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)","updated_at":"2026-05-05T03:08:30.860761453Z"}

Manual verification: 'grep -l ADR-023 docs/helix/' shows the ADR + 3 FEAT amendments.","claimed-at":"2026-05-04T14:54:43Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3540416","closing_commit_sha":"c614dd8e6bd12a69fcb5f0af1ee1d19399085971","created_at":"2026-05-04T03:21:20.818354523Z","description":"PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0: bead-lifecycle quality framework needs an ADR documenting the policy + amendments to existing FEATs to formally adopt the lint/triage hooks and the sub-skill packaging change.

ROOT CAUSE
Today no policy artifact exists for bead-quality enforcement. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 documents P7 as a principle but does not specify the enforcement mechanism, waiver model, staged rollout, or recovery UX. Existing FEAT docs are stale on relevant points:
- FEAT-011 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:180 says DDx ships exactly one skill, but .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md already demonstrates nested sub-skill packaging.
- FEAT-004 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:150 owns bead validation hooks but doesn't describe authoring-quality lint or post-attempt triage.
- FEAT-010 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:21 documents the layer-1/2/3 invocation model but doesn't spec hook insertion points for ddx try / ddx work.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Author ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md covering:
   - Policy: lint pre-dispatch + triage post-attempt; both invoke the same bead-lifecycle sub-skill
   - Staged rollout: WARN-ONLY default → BLOCK opt-in after baseline confirms
   - Waiver model: rubric-level by bead-type (doc/epic/deletion skip certain criteria, per docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:219); per-bead lint-waiver:<criterion> label for rare exceptions; --force --reason records an event (does not silently bypass)
   - Fail-open hook behavior (P1 from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES): infrastructure failure proceeds; only valid low lint score blocks (in BLOCK mode)
   - Operator recovery UX: blocked dispatch prints missing fields + suggested ddx bead update commands

2. Amend FEAT-004 (beads): add §"Authoring quality lint" describing the rubric, sub-skill location, waiver storage (label-based), and tracker metadata (no new schema fields; lint output ephemeral in evidence dir).

3. Amend FEAT-010 (executions): add §"Quality hooks" describing PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook insertion points in ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime; OutcomeReason field beside existing Disrupted in ExecuteBeadReport.

4. Amend FEAT-011 (skills): replace "ships exactly one skill" with the nested workflow-skill model demonstrated by bead-breakdown/, replay-bead/, etc. Document the 5 shipped-skill paths per codex review (skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).

5. Cross-link: ADR-023 references RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652) + evidence (ddx-f339c399) + bead-authoring-template.md as the canonical template.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT (codex: not warranted; existing FEATs cover the surface)

796-{"acceptance":"ddx init adds or updates documented .gitignore entries for DDx runtime artifacts and provider-local state that should remain untracked; the behavior is idempotent and does not mask durable tracked DDx files; provider-local config/state directories created by agent execution are ignored by default; and automated coverage verifies ignore-file insertion and deduplication","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7edacc7224bff0edc80f0b1195ea696b7a4bd3b5","created_at":"2026-04-10T19:53:14.290755858Z","description":"<context-digest>
Review area: repo bootstrap hygiene for DDx-managed and provider-local runtime state. Evidence covers current execute-bead worktree churn under .ddx/, missing ignore coverage for beads.lock and .execute-bead-wt-* paths, and the absence of any gitignore management in ddx init.
</context-digest>
Teach `ddx init` to manage ignore rules for local DDx runtime artifacts and provider-local configuration/state that should not be committed.

## Scope
- Define the canonical ignore entries DDx should add or maintain during init
- Cover DDx runtime artifacts like execute-bead worktrees and lock state
- Cover local provider state/config directories that DDx may create or depend on during agent execution and that should remain untracked
- Keep tracked project state intact: do not hide durable files like .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/config.yaml, or checked-in skill/docs content
- Make the init behavior idempotent and safe for existing .gitignore files
- Add tests covering insertion and non-duplication of managed ignore entries","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"checkpointing dirty worktree: git stash: error: could not write index

798:{"acceptance":"1. All 4 child beads closed.
2. ADR-023 status = Accepted.
3. FEAT-004, FEAT-010, FEAT-011 amendments committed.
4. Sub-skill .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md exists in all 5 shipped paths (codex: skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).
5. ddx try hooks fire in WARN-ONLY mode by default; BLOCK mode opt-in via config.
6. Baseline lint score distribution recorded post-migration; threshold for BLOCK mode chosen empirically.
7. Closed via final operator review confirming framework is operational.","created_at":"2026-05-04T03:18:36.753195174Z","description":"PROBLEM
Per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 P7 ('bead = sufficient sub-agent prompt'), bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success. Audit-1 (commit 86848d21) covered 20 beads; audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) covered 108 beads; 88 of 108 scored ≤5 against the rubric. Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap.

ROOT CAUSE
Today nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all.

PROPOSED FRAMEWORK
A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill.

NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code.

CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases)
1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy)
2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples
3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field
4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline)

After child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking)
- ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model)
- in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality)

GOVERNANCE
- ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/)
- FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks)
- FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records
- FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists)
- FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed)","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-05-04T21:51:30.735535478Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"exit status 1

("feat(web): label-aware collision and bounded convergence in doc-graph
[ddx-a9edd569]"), merged as 816752ef-predecessor b3da4924. No source changes
are required in this attempt.

The prior review BLOCK note ("Diff contains only an execution result.json
receipt; no changes to D3Graph.svelte or any source files") was incorrect —
the reviewer appears to have inspected only an attempt's evidence directory
rather than the merged commit. `git show 47b48d38 --stat` confirms 151 lines
added to cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte and
131 lines added to cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts.

Acceptance criteria, with evidence at HEAD:

AC1 — Custom label-aware collision force lands in D3Graph.svelte.
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:80-169
    `labelAwareCollide<N>(padding)` builds per-node axis-aligned bounding
    boxes covering the r=18 circle plus the trailing label (rendered at
    x=24, truncated at 32 chars + ellipsis) and resolves overlaps in a
    two-pass quadtree sweep with position correction. Wired in at line 284
    via .force('collide', labelAwareCollide<SimNode>(8)), replacing the
    earlier forceCollide(48) circle-only force.

AC2 — No two node circles overlap after settle on the 128-node fixture
    (Playwright bounding-box check).
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts:612-697 TC-043. Builds a
    128-node ternary-tree fixture with hub cross-links, waits for the
    bounded-convergence freeze (data-settleMs), enumerates every circle's
    consolidated transform, and asserts dist(i,j) >= r_i + r_j - 0.5 for
    all pairs.

AC3 — Label bounding boxes do not overlap other nodes' circles after
    settle.
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts:699-718 TC-043. For each
    node's text bbox, computes the closest point to every foreign circle
    centre and asserts the squared distance is >= (r - 0.5)^2.

AC4 — Settle completes within 2 s (reported, not gated, per codex).
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:391-400
    captures performance.now() at simulation start, and on the `end` event
    pins all nodes (fx/fy = x/y) and writes Math.round(elapsedMs) to
    svgNode.dataset.settleMs. The Playwright test reports the value via
    console.log at e2e/graph.spec.ts:722 and does not gate on it. Tuning
    that drives ~2 s settle: alphaDecay(0.05), velocityDecay(0.5),
    degree-tuned forceManyBody (-450 - 90·min(deg,10)) and forceLink
    distance (140 + 18·min(degSrc+degTgt,10)).

Drag-end semantics (from the description, not numbered AC) match codex's
recommendation:
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:338-342
    on('end') sets d.fx = null; d.fy = null (release, not re-pin).

No commit is produced because the bead contract is already satisfied at
HEAD; producing an empty or cosmetic commit would only add noise to the
merge history.

NO CHANGES RATIONALE
====================

The implementation is already present at the base revision (9cc24280) of this
worktree. It was landed by an earlier attempt in commit 07b6c67e
"feat(server): operatorPromptSubmit GraphQL mutation with CSRF + idempotency +
identity audit [ddx-f171a715]" and merged to main in 4d6db126.

The previous review BLOCK ("diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no schema, resolver, CSRF, idempotency, audit, or test code is
present") inspected an attempt diff that did not actually carry the code. The
code in fact merged and is currently present at HEAD.

Evidence each AC is satisfied
-----------------------------

AC1. operatorPromptSubmit mutation in schema.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2965 declares the mutation.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:1261,1271 generated input/result types.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go:169
    `(*mutationResolver).OperatorPromptSubmit` is the resolver.

AC2. CSRF required (returns 403 without).
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:162 errCSRF carries "(status: 403)".
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:180-189 rejects when the request has no
    *http.Request in ctx, missing header, or token mismatch.
  - StaticCSRFTokenStore.Validate (line 148) is constant-time.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_CSRF (3 subcases — missing/wrong/valid).

AC3. Idempotency key dedupes within 24h window.
  - OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL = 24h (resolver_operator_prompt.go:23).
  - MemoryIdempotencyCache.Lookup/Store enforce TTL eviction (lines 92-118).
  - Resolver short-circuits on cache hit returning Deduplicated=true (lines
    197-207) and stores key->beadID after successful create (line 237).
  - Tests: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_Idempotency (same key dedupes, different
    keys do not, no second audit event on dedup); TestMemoryIdempotencyCache_
    TTLEviction (locks 24h boundary at 23h present / 25h evicted).

AC4. Audit event includes identity.
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:218-235 appends a BeadEvent of kind
    "operator_prompt_submit" whose Body records identity kind, RemoteAddr,
    X-Tailscale-User, X-Tailscale-Node, and idempotency_key, and whose Actor
    is set from operatorPromptIdentity (tsnet user OR localhost:<remote>).
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts EventsByKind returns
    exactly one event with non-empty Actor and Body containing identity= and
    idempotency_key=key-1.

AC5. Default = proposed (not ready).
  - bead.NewOperatorPromptBead (internal/bead/types.go:163) sets
    Status = StatusProposed and IssueType = IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts Status == StatusProposed
    and IssueType == IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.

AC6. Tests cover all five.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt_test.go has four
    tests (HappyPath, CSRF with 3 subtests, Idempotency, TTLEviction) which
    together exercise AC1–AC5.

Verification
------------

  $ go test ./internal/server/graphql/ -run 'OperatorPrompt|MemoryIdempotency' -v -count=1
  === RUN   TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath
  --- PASS: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath
  === RUN   TestOperatorPromptSubmit_CSRF
      --- PASS: missing_header_rejected
      --- PASS: wrong_token_rejected
      --- PASS: valid_token_accepted

rationale: Bead ddx-f171a715 — operatorPromptSubmit mutation + CSRF + idempotency + identity-bound audit

NO CHANGES RATIONALE
====================

The implementation is already present at the base revision (9cc24280) of this
worktree. It was landed by an earlier attempt in commit 07b6c67e
"feat(server): operatorPromptSubmit GraphQL mutation with CSRF + idempotency +
identity audit [ddx-f171a715]" and merged to main in 4d6db126.

The previous review BLOCK ("diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no schema, resolver, CSRF, idempotency, audit, or test code is
present") inspected an attempt diff that did not actually carry the code. The
code in fact merged and is currently present at HEAD.

Evidence each AC is satisfied
-----------------------------

AC1. operatorPromptSubmit mutation in schema.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2965 declares the mutation.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:1261,1271 generated input/result types.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go:169
    `(*mutationResolver).OperatorPromptSubmit` is the resolver.

AC2. CSRF required (returns 403 without).
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:162 errCSRF carries "(status: 403)".
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:180-189 rejects when the request has no
    *http.Request in ctx, missing header, or token mismatch.
  - StaticCSRFTokenStore.Validate (line 148) is constant-time.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_CSRF (3 subcases — missing/wrong/valid).

AC3. Idempotency key dedupes within 24h window.
  - OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL = 24h (resolver_operator_prompt.go:23).
  - MemoryIdempotencyCache.Lookup/Store enforce TTL eviction (lines 92-118).
  - Resolver short-circuits on cache hit returning Deduplicated=true (lines
    197-207) and stores key->beadID after successful create (line 237).
  - Tests: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_Idempotency (same key dedupes, different
    keys do not, no second audit event on dedup); TestMemoryIdempotencyCache_
    TTLEviction (locks 24h boundary at 23h present / 25h evicted).

AC4. Audit event includes identity.
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:218-235 appends a BeadEvent of kind
    "operator_prompt_submit" whose Body records identity kind, RemoteAddr,
    X-Tailscale-User, X-Tailscale-Node, and idempotency_key, and whose Actor
    is set from operatorPromptIdentity (tsnet user OR localhost:<remote>).
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts EventsByKind returns
    exactly one event with non-empty Actor and Body containing identity= and
    idempotency_key=key-1.

AC5. Default = proposed (not ready).
  - bead.NewOperatorPromptBead (internal/bead/types.go:163) sets
    Status = StatusProposed and IssueType = IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts Status == StatusProposed
    and IssueType == IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.

AC6. Tests cover all five.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt_test.go has four
    tests (HappyPath, CSRF with 3 subtests, Idempotency, TTLEviction) which
    together exercise AC1–AC5.

Verification
------------

Review sessions have no cost cap enforcement, no reviewer routing policy, and no structured refusal contracts. Without these, a review session can run unbounded cost, use an unavailable reviewer, or fail with an unstructured error that the frontend cannot surface to the operator.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/review_session.go (from ddx-e7b80a50, a dep): ReviewSession has max_billable_usd field but no enforcement code.
- No reviewer routing logic exists in the server review path — preferred_reviewer from Story 17 and the escalation ladder from Story 10 are not consulted.
- No structured refusal error codes exist (PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE).
- ddx-0e5c3005 (reviewer routing contract, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Enforce max_billable_usd per session: before each turn, check cumulative cost_usd against max_billable_usd; if exceeded, return COST_CAP_EXCEEDED refusal.
- Reviewer routing: consult Story 17's preferred_reviewer first; fall back to Story 10's escalation ladder.
- Downgrade routing: when preferred reviewer is unavailable, downgrade to next ladder rung and emit review-pairing-degraded event.
- Implement three structured refusal error codes as typed errors with structured JSON body.

NON-SCOPE
- Turn dispatcher implementation (ddx-f7c3d512, a dep).

'ddx try <ready-bead-id>' claims the bead, executes via the existing execute-bead-loop machinery (one iteration, one bead), writes evidence to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/, and exits with the bead's terminal status code (0 = success/already_satisfied; 1 = preserved_for_review/no_changes; 2 = execution_failed).

6. Flag plumbing: --harness, --model, --profile, --provider, --effort, --opaque-passthrough, --no-review, --review-harness, --review-model. Each flag, when set, plumbs identically to the corresponding 'ddx work' flag (same struct fields in the runtime config).

7. WIRED-IN: the new 'ddx try' command is registered in cli/cmd/root.go (or wherever the cobra root command assembles subcommands). Verified by 'ddx try --help' output AND by 'git grep "tryCmd\|NewTryCommand" cli/cmd/' showing the registration.

Manual verification (operator runs after merge): 'ddx try ddx-50da9674' (closed fixture-repo bead) returns non-zero with the closed-bead message; 'ddx try <some-ready-bead>' actually claims and executes it.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T20:06:45Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"ff5e64cecc17f3094f3933986966b94a50dc4f59","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:06:37.62309311Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.
- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep order.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:33:19.857924528Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T200645-f1713f26","harness":"claude","input_tokens":112,"output_tokens":52083,"total_tokens":52195,"cost_usd":8.31247625,"duration_ms":1590247,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:33:19.956008288Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=52195 cost_usd=8.3125"},{"actor":"erik","body":"preserve_ref=refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-f261d2a0/20260503T200645-f1713f26-88dde5dc9001

rationale: status: already_satisfied

Every AC item for ddx-f3c7149d (S15-4) is already implemented and merged.
The REVIEW:BLOCK note on this bead is stale: it describes an earlier
attempt whose diff contained only an execution result.json. A later
attempt landed the actual code in commit 7c1ff6a5
("feat(graphql): enforce per-project allowlist on ts-net operator-prompt
submit [ddx-f3c7149d]") and merge commit 804f6dcf, both of which are
already in HEAD's history (base 75e0d9472387 includes them).

AC mapping (all in cli/internal/server/graphql/):

1. Approve mutation transitions proposed -> open (trusted only):
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:398 OperatorPromptApprove
     - requires CSRF, requires canAutoApprove(ident) for the caller
     - rejects non-operator-prompt or non-proposed beads
     - sets bead.StatusOpen and appends OperatorPromptApproveEventKind
   Test: resolver_operator_prompt_lifecycle_test.go
     TestOperatorPromptApprove_TransitionsProposedToOpen

2. Cancel transitions proposed -> cancelled:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:446 OperatorPromptCancel
     - sets bead.StatusCancelled and appends OperatorPromptCancelEventKind
   Test: TestOperatorPromptCancel_TransitionsProposedToCancelled

3. auto-approve config skips proposed for configured localhost identity
   only and never for ts-net by default:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:533 canAutoApprove
     - returns false unconditionally when ident.kind != "localhost"
     - empty allowlist -> false; "localhost" sentinel matches any actor
     OperatorPromptSubmit branch at resolver_operator_prompt.go:361 only
     calls canAutoApprove when input.AutoApprove=true.
   Tests:
     TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet
     TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost
     TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedWhenIdentityNotOnAllowlist

4. Per-project allowlist enforced; non-listed ts-net peers get 403 on
   submit:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:513 canSubmit
     - localhost always allowed; ts-net only when actor in allowlist
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:233 errSubmitDenied (status 403)
   Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetIdentityNotInAllowlistDenied

5. Config wiring:
     server/server.go:116-170 operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist field +
     parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv(DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST)
     server/server.go:4709 plumbed into Resolver
     SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist setter for tests

75-{"acceptance":"1) TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-24T05:04:15Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"98a6cd10ca81c2db13e46aad73e3ed6a02a72de4","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:42.554923255Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0629ed12","depends_on_id":"ddx-915240dd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:42:24Z"}],"description":"Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].","closing_commit_sha":"baf5f748920779c3379712225341fe58ec9be46c","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.651384066Z","description":"The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.","events_attachment":"ddx-06b77652/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"exit status 1","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-05-05T04:20:59Z","id":"ddx-06b77652","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:server","kind:design","reliability","bead-quality"],"notes":"Evidence cross-link: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and serves as evidence for P7 / bead-quality.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-249cd7a8","status":"closed","title":"ADR-style: 7 reliability principles for ddx try / ddx work / sub-agent execution","updated_at":"2026-05-05T14:19:39.552645934Z"}

7. TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce: nil ComplexityGate → one warning, allow proceeds.

10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.181053449Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","depends_on_id":"ddx-b669bb9f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.","id":"ddx-06eb05d8","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result","updated_at":"2026-05-05T07:43:36.356850587Z"}

Several page-data tests in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` fail because the default Playwright harness previously served static preview output with no backend data. Once the Go-server fixture harness is in place, TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, and TC-007 should navigate through `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...` and assert against the seeded fixture documents, beads, personas, and graph data instead of assuming developer-local state.

Rewrite only those TC blocks to use the fixture-backed app and current node/project router. Preserve the intended coverage of the existing TC IDs; update selectors and data expectations only where the current UI and fixture data require it.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` — TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, and TC-007 only.

## Out-of-scope

- TC-001 and TC-008 in `e2e/app.spec.ts`.
- Other e2e spec files.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and fixture seeding except for reading fixture IDs/data created by the prerequisite bead.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts` and `playwright.demo.config.ts`.
- Frontend source changes under `src/` or Go server changes under `cli/internal/server/`.

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads
- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package layout","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.009514865Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T021424-dac100b6","harness":"claude","input_tokens":21,"output_tokens":14799,"total_tokens":14820,"cost_usd":1.0767455,"duration_ms":249728,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.0358447Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=14820 cost_usd=1.0767"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:43.263235583Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"APPROVE

265:{"acceptance":"1. 5 fixture beads chosen and listed in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/comparison-fixtures.md with: bead ID, base-rev, original closing commit, characterization (trivial/refactor/test/multi-file/docs).
2. Path A run: each fixture's bead replayed via ddx try (or ddx work --once) on a branch from base-rev~1; outcomes captured.
3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.
4. Path C run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with operator hand-curated prompt; outcomes captured.
5. Per-fixture, per-path measurements table with the 7 named metrics.
6. Aggregate stats + failure-mode distribution.
7. Hypothesis verdict section: H1 / H2 / both / inconclusive.
8. Recommendations section: which path for which bead-shape.
9. Report committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md.
10. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.880567652Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-358f2457","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c8f79963","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-b669bb9f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-848069a3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-9228a484","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-0b8780db","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c670ef0a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-387a0178","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"}],"description":"Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. ## Scope
- Define an explicit execute-bead prompt template / rationalizer contract
- Define tracked execution artifacts for each attempt, separate from ignored local runtime scratch
- Define which execution evidence is committed on landed and preserved attempts

Manual verification: 'grep -l ADR-023 docs/helix/' shows the ADR + 3 FEAT amendments.","claimed-at":"2026-05-04T14:54:43Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3540416","closing_commit_sha":"c614dd8e6bd12a69fcb5f0af1ee1d19399085971","created_at":"2026-05-04T03:21:20.818354523Z","description":"PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0: bead-lifecycle quality framework needs an ADR documenting the policy + amendments to existing FEATs to formally adopt the lint/triage hooks and the sub-skill packaging change.

ROOT CAUSE
Today no policy artifact exists for bead-quality enforcement. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 documents P7 as a principle but does not specify the enforcement mechanism, waiver model, staged rollout, or recovery UX. Existing FEAT docs are stale on relevant points:
- FEAT-011 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:180 says DDx ships exactly one skill, but .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md already demonstrates nested sub-skill packaging.
- FEAT-004 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:150 owns bead validation hooks but doesn't describe authoring-quality lint or post-attempt triage.
- FEAT-010 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:21 documents the layer-1/2/3 invocation model but doesn't spec hook insertion points for ddx try / ddx work.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Author ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md covering:
   - Policy: lint pre-dispatch + triage post-attempt; both invoke the same bead-lifecycle sub-skill
   - Staged rollout: WARN-ONLY default → BLOCK opt-in after baseline confirms
   - Waiver model: rubric-level by bead-type (doc/epic/deletion skip certain criteria, per docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:219); per-bead lint-waiver:<criterion> label for rare exceptions; --force --reason records an event (does not silently bypass)
   - Fail-open hook behavior (P1 from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES): infrastructure failure proceeds; only valid low lint score blocks (in BLOCK mode)
   - Operator recovery UX: blocked dispatch prints missing fields + suggested ddx bead update commands

2. Amend FEAT-004 (beads): add §"Authoring quality lint" describing the rubric, sub-skill location, waiver storage (label-based), and tracker metadata (no new schema fields; lint output ephemeral in evidence dir).

3. Amend FEAT-010 (executions): add §"Quality hooks" describing PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook insertion points in ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime; OutcomeReason field beside existing Disrupted in ExecuteBeadReport.

4. Amend FEAT-011 (skills): replace "ships exactly one skill" with the nested workflow-skill model demonstrated by bead-breakdown/, replay-bead/, etc. Document the 5 shipped-skill paths per codex review (skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).

5. Cross-link: ADR-023 references RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652) + evidence (ddx-f339c399) + bead-authoring-template.md as the canonical template.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT (codex: not warranted; existing FEATs cover the surface)
- ADR for the in_progress-eligibility bug (separate concern)
- Cross-project skill packaging (deferred per FEAT-015)
- Schema changes to bead JSON (waivers via labels; no new fields)","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"pre-execute-bead checkpoint: staging changes: exit status 128","created_at":"2026-05-04T14:44:19.4585742Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"execution_failed"},{"actor":"erik","body":"creating isolated worktree: git worktree add: Preparing worktree (detached HEAD fb72b7a7)

Execute-loop cost-cap and stop logic is scattered across executor closure in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 1722-1730, 1818-1850) and ad-hoc loop continuation checks. Stopping conditions (Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked) are evaluated implicitly with no enum, making the stop logic brittle and hard to extend. The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) exists in cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go but has no production caller — dead-code audit (ddx-09d2990c) must confirm before this child can safely wire or delete it.

ROOT CAUSE
- Cost cap: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722-1730 checks max_cost inside executor closure; cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818-1850 has a second cost-cap site. Neither site uses a StopCondition type.
- Escalation: cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30 defines Ladder.Next; no call site in production confirmed (dead-code audit required per ddx-09d2990c).
- Reviewer cost (from ddx-a8718bec): reviewer cost is tracked in a separate accumulator inside drain but not evaluated in a unified stop gate.
- Worker stay-alive tactical (from ddx-dc157075): poll-interval default is 0 (drain mode); the tactical default of 30s should come from C10 (ddx-f9acb86d) but cost-cap relocation must happen first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defining StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked.
- Add drain.stop(state DrainState) StopCondition — evaluates all stop conditions including cost cap.
- Remove cost cap from executor closure (agent_cmd.go:1722-1730, 1818-1850); move into drain.stop budget evaluation.
- Relocate ddx-cfedee8e MinPower-ladder behavior into try.Attempt Retry path (wire Ladder.Next or confirm deletion from dead-code audit).
- Relocate ddx-a8718bec reviewer cost accumulator into drain.stop budget evaluation.
- Subsume ddx-dc157075 tactical changes.
- After cost-cap consumers audit: find all dashboard/alerting consumers of execution_failed with max-cost reason; document in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.

NON-SCOPE
- Poll-interval default (30s) belongs to C10 (ddx-f9acb86d).
- Frontend changes.
- Removal of ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-a8718bec / ddx-dc157075 beads (operator closes those after confirming subsumption).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: stop conditions should fail open into a bounded stop state, not a worker wedge.
- P2: StopCondition owns stop classification, not unrelated eligibility or routing policy.
- P3: budget and max-cost transitions should be observable.
- P4: cost-cap behavior must stay local to the affected run.
- P5: the worker state should remain visible while it is claiming, executing, or blocked.
- P6: cooldown must stay limited to transient no-progress classes.","id":"ddx-9228a484","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"C9: first-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation (absorbs ddx-cfedee8e + ddx-a8718bec + ddx-dc157075 behavior)","updated_at":"2026-05-05T07:43:37.227044785Z"}

Teach `ddx init` to manage ignore rules for local DDx runtime artifacts and provider-local configuration/state that should not be committed.

## Scope
- Define the canonical ignore entries DDx should add or maintain during init

- Keep tracked project state intact: do not hide durable files like .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/config.yaml, or checked-in skill/docs content

2. ADR-023 status = Accepted.

7. Closed via final operator review confirming framework is operational.","created_at":"2026-05-04T03:18:36.753195174Z","description":"PROBLEM
Per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 P7 ('bead = sufficient sub-agent prompt'), bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success. Audit-1 (commit 86848d21) covered 20 beads; audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) covered 108 beads; 88 of 108 scored ≤5 against the rubric. Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap.

ROOT CAUSE
Today nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all.

PROPOSED FRAMEWORK
A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill.

NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code.

CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases)
1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy)
2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples
3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field
4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline)

After child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking)
- ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model)
- in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality)

GOVERNANCE
- ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/)
- FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks)
- FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records
- FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists)
- FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed)","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-05-04T21:51:30.735535478Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"exit status 1

The prior review BLOCK note ("Diff contains only an execution result.json
receipt; no changes to D3Graph.svelte or any source files") was incorrect —
the reviewer appears to have inspected only an attempt's evidence directory
rather than the merged commit. `git show 47b48d38 --stat` confirms 151 lines
added to cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte and
131 lines added to cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts.

Acceptance criteria, with evidence at HEAD:

AC1 — Custom label-aware collision force lands in D3Graph.svelte.
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:80-169
    `labelAwareCollide<N>(padding)` builds per-node axis-aligned bounding
    boxes covering the r=18 circle plus the trailing label (rendered at
    x=24, truncated at 32 chars + ellipsis) and resolves overlaps in a
    two-pass quadtree sweep with position correction. Wired in at line 284
    via .force('collide', labelAwareCollide<SimNode>(8)), replacing the
    earlier forceCollide(48) circle-only force.

AC2 — No two node circles overlap after settle on the 128-node fixture
    (Playwright bounding-box check).
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts:612-697 TC-043. Builds a
    128-node ternary-tree fixture with hub cross-links, waits for the
    bounded-convergence freeze (data-settleMs), enumerates every circle's
    consolidated transform, and asserts dist(i,j) >= r_i + r_j - 0.5 for
    all pairs.

AC3 — Label bounding boxes do not overlap other nodes' circles after
    settle.
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts:699-718 TC-043. For each
    node's text bbox, computes the closest point to every foreign circle
    centre and asserts the squared distance is >= (r - 0.5)^2.

AC4 — Settle completes within 2 s (reported, not gated, per codex).
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:391-400
    captures performance.now() at simulation start, and on the `end` event
    pins all nodes (fx/fy = x/y) and writes Math.round(elapsedMs) to
    svgNode.dataset.settleMs. The Playwright test reports the value via
    console.log at e2e/graph.spec.ts:722 and does not gate on it. Tuning
    that drives ~2 s settle: alphaDecay(0.05), velocityDecay(0.5),
    degree-tuned forceManyBody (-450 - 90·min(deg,10)) and forceLink
    distance (140 + 18·min(degSrc+degTgt,10)).

Drag-end semantics (from the description, not numbered AC) match codex's
recommendation:
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:338-342
    on('end') sets d.fx = null; d.fy = null (release, not re-pin).

No commit is produced because the bead contract is already satisfied at
HEAD; producing an empty or cosmetic commit would only add noise to the
merge history.

801-{"acceptance":"New test TestRunWorker_ScriptHarness_LandsCommit in cli/internal/server/workers_test.go (or cli/internal/server/workers_land_test.go) that uses the script harness + integration helper to spawn one server-managed worker, waits for it to exit, and asserts: (1) record.LastResult.ResultRev matches `git log main -1 --format=%H` on the temp repo, (2) main has exactly one new commit from the initial seed, (3) the bead is closed with closing_commit_sha=record.LastResult.ResultRev, (4) no refs/ddx/iterations/ refs were created on the success path, (5) `git log --merges main | wc -l` is 0. Test must fail against current workers.go:runWorker (no LandBeadResult call) and pass after the fix. Fix is either (a) interim: add LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult to the workers.go:248 closure using RealOrchestratorGitOps, or (b) canonical: route through the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6. Whichever is shipped, the test locks the contract: server-managed workers MUST advance the target branch. Runs under `go test ./internal/server -run RunWorker_ScriptHarness -race -count=1` in under 15s.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:50:53.571128557Z","description":"The server path at cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker (around line 237) spawns worker goroutines whose inline executor closure (around line 248) calls agent.ExecuteBead directly and returns an ExecuteBeadReport — but never calls agent.LandBeadResult or any land coordinator. As a result, every commit produced by a server-managed execute-loop worker stays in its isolated worktree and never reaches the target branch. The loop reports success, closes the bead in the tracker, and the worktree is cleaned up — but `git log main` never sees the commit.

This is the parent bead ddx-e14efc58's bug. This child bead is specifically the REGRESSION TEST that locks the fix in place, and it depends on the script harness (ddx-025046ac) + integration helper (ddx-6f6bf12a) being in place.

## What this bead builds

A regression test in cli/internal/server/workers_test.go (or a new file cli/internal/server/workers_land_test.go) that:

1. Uses the integration helper from ddx-6f6bf12a to create a real temp git repo with a seeded bead
2. Instantiates a WorkerManager rooted at the temp repo
3. Submits ONE server-managed execute-loop worker with `--harness=script` and a directive file that appends a line and commits
4. Waits for runWorker to exit (via the existing signaling mechanism — close(progressCh), progressDone)
5. Asserts:
   - record.LastResult.ResultRev is non-empty and matches the script harness's commit SHA
   - `git log main -1 --format=%H` on the temp repo equals record.LastResult.ResultRev
   - `git rev-list main ^initial | wc -l` equals 1
   - ddx bead show returns status=closed with closing_commit_sha=record.LastResult.ResultRev
   - NO refs/ddx/iterations/* refs were created
   - `git log --merges main | wc -l` is 0 (linear history invariant)

The test MUST fail against today's workers.go:runWorker (which does not call LandBeadResult) and MUST pass after the fix. This catches the regression if anyone later re-introduces the gap.

## Fix scope

The fix is either:
- **Interim (smaller)**: mirror the --local closure at agent_cmd.go:1372 by calling LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult inside the workers.go:248 closure, using RealOrchestratorGitOps. This uses the current merge-based path and produces 2-parent merge commits, but at least the work lands.
- **Canonical (larger)**: use the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6 once it exists — workers submit to the coordinator, coordinator rebases + ff-pushes, history stays linear. This is the meta-bead's design.

Prefer the canonical path if ddx-8746d8a6 has landed by the time this bead is worked. Otherwise ship the interim and note that ddx-8746d8a6 will replace it.

## Depends on

- ddx-025046ac (script harness) — the test uses it
- ddx-6f6bf12a (integration helper) — the test uses it
- ddx-8746d8a6 OR the interim LandBeadResult wiring — the production fix

## Out of scope

- Rewriting the entire workers.go runWorker (that's ddx-8746d8a6)
- Multi-project server tests (a separate concern)
- Client-side CLI changes (the CLI already handles local path correctly)","id":"ddx-aa6cc0e5","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:server","area:testing","kind:bug","phase:build"],"parent":"ddx-e14efc58","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"Server runWorker: land commits to target branch + script-harness regression test","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.735586575Z"}
